name: contributing
context: Contribution guide - create, validate, and submit Dojo nodes through GitHub
info: Practical guide for contributing new Dojo manifests. Covers initialization, rich knowledge content, entry-versus-inline script choices, the dedicated `dojo/author` authoring skill, validation, and opening GitHub pull requests with `gh`.
triggers: how do i contribute to dojo
how do i open a dojo pr
how do i contribute to dojo on github
how do i validate a dojo manifest
what is the dojo contribution flow
body:
# Contributing to Dojo

Recommended flow:
- run `dojo init` or use [[dojo/author#workflow]] to scaffold and package a new node
- fill in `context`, `info`, `tags`, `triggers`, and any executable `scripts`
- add rich docs fields like `aliases`, `body`, `sections`, `links`, and `related` when the node teaches or explains something
- prefer `entry` scripts once the logic no longer fits comfortably inline
- run [[dojo/validate]] and check [[dojo/validate/checklist#entry-and-tests]]
- commit the change on a branch and open a pull request with `gh pr create --fill`
- keep the PR description and diff focused so review is about the manifest quality, not unrelated churn

A new top-level ecosystem should start with `nodes/<ecosystem>/node.json`. Legacy sample ecosystems may still live under `examples/` with `skill.json`, but the live Dojo tree in `nodes/dojo/` is the canonical reference shape.

sections.title: Quickstart
sections.body: Use `dojo init my-skill --type skill --parent my-ecosystem/my-standard` or [[dojo/author#workflow]] to scaffold a manifest. Fill in the short decision text first, then the execution and knowledge fields.

sections.title: Rich docs fields
sections.body: Do not stop at `context` and `info` for documentation-heavy nodes. Add `aliases` for natural phrasing, `body` for the overview, `sections` for structured reading, `links` for explicit graph edges, and `related` for softer semantic relationships. See [[dojo/knowledge#authoring-patterns]] for the why.

sections.title: Entry versus inline
sections.body: Inline scripts are fine for short, local examples. Move to `entry` files once the logic becomes large enough that it deserves comments, tests, or reuse across related skills. The live Dojo node tree uses entry files for the query and validation skills so the manifest remains readable.

sections.title: GitHub pull request flow
sections.body: Create a focused branch, commit the manifest changes, push it, and open a draft or ready pull request with `gh pr create --fill`. Use the PR as the review surface: include validation results, explain why the node belongs where it does, and keep follow-up edits on the same branch with `gh pr edit` when the summary needs to change.

sections.title: Review hygiene
sections.body: A good review checks more than JSON validity. Reviewers should inspect alias quality, section usefulness, graph connectivity, lifecycle metadata, and whether the node gives an agent a clear next step. That is why [[dojo/validate/knowledge]] exists beside schema validation.
